name: openEuler ai SIG
description: Build an AI infrastructure framework to provide key capabilities such as unified access of southbound XPUs (such as CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs), heterogeneous converged memory, heterogeneous converged virtualization, computing power segmentation, training resource priority configuration, and elastic migration. Enables full-stack adaptation of AI applications and helps enterprises build AI infrastructure solutions in scenarios such as training and promotion acceleration and hybrid deployment of training and promotion.

name: openEuler ai SIG Repository List
description: openEuler ai SIG maintains 68 repositories in the AI and Machine Learning domain

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler ai SIG (Special Interest Group) is responsible for Build an AI infrastructure framework to provide key capabilities such as unified access of southbound XPUs (such as CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs), heterogeneous converged memory, heterogeneous converged virtualization, computing power segmentation, training resource priority configuration, and elastic migration. Enables full-stack adaptation of AI applications and helps enterprises build AI infrastructure solutions in scenarios such as training and promotion acceleration and hybrid deployment of training and promotion.. The SIG has 13 core members and maintains 68 repositories in the AI and Machine Learning domain.

mainEntity.name: How to participate in openEuler ai SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: To participate in openEuler ai SIG: 1) Submit Pull Requests to relevant repositories; 2) Subscribe to the mailing list at ai-infra@openeuler.org; 3) Attend SIG regular meetings, see the openEuler website calendar for details.

mainEntity.name: What repositories does openEuler ai SIG include?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler ai SIG maintains 68 repositories. Full list at https://www.openeuler.org/en/sig/ai.

mainEntity.name: Who are the Maintainers of openEuler ai SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler ai SIG has 5 Maintainers, including: dukaitian, zhuweixi, zhengxian. Maintainers are responsible for final code review and merging.
